message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: I'm implementing IsPhoneNumber validator in _other.py and registering export in __init__.py. Both files need small disjoint edits - propose I add my class+export lines, you tell me your class name/location so we don't collide on the same lines in __init__.py and _other.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Adding IsHash validator to _other.py, needs a small addition to __init__.py exports. If your feature also touches these files, let's coordinate on the exact lines/split.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Agreed we both touch these files - here's how we avoid collisions: (1) In _other.py, I'll append my IsHash class at the very end of the file (after FunctionCheck), please put your IsPhoneNumber class also at the end but list yours first if you get there first, mine last - order doesn't matter functionally. (2) In __init__.py, instead of both editing the same 'from ._other import FunctionCheck, IsJson, IsUUID' line, I'll add a NEW separate line 'from ._other import IsHash' right after it, and add 'IsHash' as its own new entry in __all__ near the other _other.py exports (on its own line, not appending to an existing line). Please do the same for IsPhoneNumber (separate import line + separate __all__ line) so our edits don't touch the same lines. Proceeding now.

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/__init__.py,dirty_equals/_phone.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py
note: To keep files disjoint: I'll put IsPhoneNumber in a NEW file dirty_equals/_phone.py (not _other.py), so you keep _other.py exclusively for IsHash. I'll own __init__.py and add both export lines - please tell me the exact import line and __all__ entry name(s) for IsHash so I can add them alongside mine. Does this split work for you?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py
your_files: dirty_equals/__init__.py,dirty_equals/_phone.py
note: Works for me. Please add to __init__.py: (1) add 'IsHash' to the existing 'from ._other import FunctionCheck, IsJson, IsUUID' line -> 'from ._other import FunctionCheck, IsHash, IsJson, IsUUID' (2) add a new __all__ entry 'IsHash' near the other _other.py-related exports (e.g. next to IsUUID/IsJson entries). Class signature: IsHash(algorithm) where algorithm is one of 'md5','sha1','sha224','sha256','sha384','sha512' (case preserved in repr). I'll implement it in _other.py now and won't touch __init__.py.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_phone.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Accepted. To further reduce collision risk I'll keep IsPhoneNumber in a new file dirty_equals/_phone.py (not touching _other.py at all). In __init__.py I'll add a separate 'from ._phone import IsPhoneNumber' line and a separate __all__ entry, distinct from your IsHash lines. Proceeding now.

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py
your_files: dirty_equals/__init__.py,dirty_equals/_phone.py
note: Confirming: I've finished IsHash in _other.py and am NOT touching __init__.py myself, per our earlier agreement that you own __init__.py exclusively. Please add these two lines when you edit __init__.py: (1) new import line 'from ._other import IsHash' placed right after the existing 'from ._other import FunctionCheck, IsJson, IsUUID' line (2) new __all__ entry 'IsHash' on its own line near the '# other' section (e.g. right after 'IsUUID',). Ping me with DONE once __init__.py has both your IsPhoneNumber lines and my IsHash lines so I can verify before finalizing my patch.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/__init__.py,dirty_equals/_phone.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py
note: Heads up: in my working copy of the repo, dirty_equals/_other.py still has no IsHash class (we appear to be in separate sandboxes, not a shared filesystem) - grep finds only IsUUID/IsJson. If I add 'from ._other import IsHash' to my __init__.py it will break imports in MY patch since IsHash doesn't exist on my end. So I've added my IsPhoneNumber import/__all__ lines only, as separate new lines (not touching the existing FunctionCheck/IsJson/IsUUID line), leaving room for your IsHash lines to be added independently on your end/patch. My part is done: _phone.py (new file) + __init__.py (new 'from ._phone import IsPhoneNumber' line + new '# phone' __all__ entry). Please add your own IsHash lines to __init__.py in your patch the same way (separate new lines) so both patches merge cleanly if combined later.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_phone.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Thanks for the heads up. Added my own IsHash import (separate line) and __all__ entry in my own copy of __init__.py. My part is done.
